schema.title: Returns the successfully created auth token

accessToken.description: The auth token that can be used to create new fidentity processes or to get data from existing ones.

post.description: Before a new fidentity process can be started or data from an existing process can be read, the client must authenticate itself. Consider to reuse the token for multiple requests because this endpoint is strictly rate limited.

requestBody.description: authentication object that needs to be created

post.description: With this service you can start a new fidentity processes.

processUrl.title: The url to the fidentity process, it is only valid until the expiry date

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Date filter for process list as ISO date string

get.description: Retrieves a list of fidentity processes from the service. By default the last 5 days are returned (max 1000 processes).

parameters.name: extId
parameters.description: External unique ID for the process.

get.description: Retrieves a single fidentity with the given extId from the service.
get.summary: Gets a fidentity process by extId from the service.

parameters.name: extId
parameters.description: External unique ID for the process.

get.description: Retrieves the state of a fidentity process.

post.summary: Reset a fidentity process and Customer
post.description: With this service you can reset a existing fidentity process and the associated process with the help of the extId. The Body of the POST Request is empty

parameters.name: extId
parameters.description: External unique ID for the process.

processUrl.title: The url to the fidentity process, it is only valid until the expiry date

get.summary: get a fresh link for a created process
get.description: With this service you can get a fresh link to a existing fidentity process by the extId.

parameters.name: extId
parameters.description: External unique ID for the process.

processUrl.title: The url to the fidentity process, it is only valid until the expiry date

post.description: With this service you can send scanned images for a fidentity process by the extId.

parameters.name: extId
parameters.description: External unique ID for the process.

parameters.name: processStep
parameters.description: Type of the processStep to which images belong.

put.summary: Update document with a signed version
put.description: Update document with a signed version.

post.summary: Upload a new document for signature
post.description: Upload a new document for signature, while process is in state reviewed

type.title: type of document, values are used to distinguish the document types

flatten.title: If true, the document will be flattened. This means that all form fields will be removed and the document will be saved as a flat PDF. This is useful for signing documents that contain form fields.

oneOf.title: section to position the electronic signature

x.title: The x position from the left in user space units (distance in inch*72)

y.title: The y position from the top in user space units (distance in inch*72)

page.title: The page index, starting at 0 for first page. Use -1 to place the signature on the last page.

oneOf.title: Placeholder string to position the electronic signature. This feature is only available if "Signature" type is set in processSteps.

placeholder.title: Instead of coordinates and page number you can pass a placeholder string. The signature will be inserted at the first occurence of the placeholder in the pdf document. The placeholder in the pdf needs to be between 2 dollar signs: $$Signature-0001$$. However you must only pass the placeholder string to the request, without dollar signs. The placeholder in the pdf may also contain the desired width and height of the signature box as well as the desired signature text. Example1: $$Signature-0001,100,50$$ (Placeholdername, width, height) Example2: $$Signature-0001,100,50,Signed by John Doe$$ (Placeholdername, width, height, signature text).

visualSignature.title: section to define the visual size of the electronic signature

text.title: The text that will be displayed on the signature. Supports the following placeholders: {{date}}, {{time}},  {{datetime}} with optional format {{date | dd.MM.yyyy}}, {{time | HH:mm:ss}}, {{datetime | yy-MM-DDTHH:mm}}
text.example: Signed by fidentity (this is the default if no custom text is passed)

image.title: The base64 encoded image as Data Url (data:[<mediatype>][;base64],<data>) that will be displayed on the signature. Encoded, it should be no larger than 200Kb. Will be resized to fit the signature box. If you want the handwritten Signature as the visual Signature, you must pass an empty string and add the HandwrittenSignature Step to the processSteps array

boxSize.title: Total size of the visual signature box, containing the signature text and the image (in user space units = inches*72. For example: 1 inch = 2.54 cm = 72 user space units)

signatureType.title: Type of the signature, only relevant for QES case. Either 'sign' the document with QES or only 'seal' it. Useful for accompanying documents like 'terms and conditions' documents.

schema.title: section to define the visual size of the electronic signature

image.title: The base64 encoded image as Data Url mediatype=jpeg that will be displayed on the signature. Encoded, it should be no larger than 200Kb. Will be resized to fit the signature box

placeholderImage.title: The base64 encoded image as Data Url mediatype=jpeg that will be displayed on the signature. Encoded, it should be no larger than 200Kb. Will be resized to fit the signature box

boxSize.title: Total width of the visual signature box, containing the signature image (in user space units = inches*72. For example: 1 inch = 2.54 cm = 72 user space units)

position.title: section to position the electronic signature

x.title: The x position from the left in user space units (distance in inch*72)

y.title: The y position from the top in user space units (distance in inch*72)

page.title: The page index, starting at 0 for first page

parameters.name: passkeyUsername
parameters.description: A unique customer id, given by as parameter "passkeyUsername" when creating new fidentity process.

schema.title: section to define the visual size of the electronic signature

identificationExtId.title: ExtId of the process where the user was identified

createdAt.title: Date when the user was identified

strategy.description: The authentication strategy. Now this value is used with the default value only.

clientId.description: The id of the client. This id is issued by fidentity.

clientSecret.description: The password which belongs to the clientId. The password is issued by fidentity.

streetName.title: street, validated address of the customer

townName.title: townName, validated address of the customer

zipCode.title: zipCode, validated address of the customer

houseNo.title: houseNo, validated address of the customer

additionalChecks.title: Additional checks which were done in the review

items.title: Additional check done in the review

customData.title: optional custom data section for data that was gatherd during the process, in key-value pairs

verificationType.title: The verification type for the QES process. If not provided, the tenant default is used.

certificateType.title: The certificate type for the QES process. If not provided, the tenant default is used.

case.title: Qualifier to define the case that should be used. For QES, this field is deprecated (use process steps instead).
case.x-comment: Used for example by bank avera. But DEPRECATED for QES, use processSteps field instead.

processSteps.title: The sequence of the fidentity process steps that were planned.

signedHashes.title: section for hashes that are signed electronically

cancelReason.title: Reason why the process was cancelled (e.g. blocked because user was underage or fraud detected during review). Examples: MAXRESCANREACHED, UNDERAGE, INVALIDCOUNTRY, FRAUDDETECTEDBYREVIEW

compliances.title: list of compliances the process fulfills (identifying assessments)

verificationType.title: The verification type for the QES process. If not provided, the tenant default is used.

certificateType.title: The certificate type for the QES process. If not provided, the tenant default is used.

nationality.title: The nationality of the customer (ISO Code). This field defines which legal documents are shown to the customer. The allowed countries are defined individually when creating the projects

permit.title: The swiss residence permit of the customer

dateOfBirth.title: Date of birth of the customer

idType.title: The type of the legal document. If provided this will be used for the validation

idIssuingCountry.title: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of issuing country of the ID document. If provided this will be used for the validation

countryOfResidence.title: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of residence country

residencePermit.title: Flag to indicate if the residence permit needs to be checked.

checkAddress.title: Flag to indicate if the address of the customer needs to be checked.

passkeyUsername.title: A unique customer id which will be used as passkey (e.g. Webauthn) for signing. If it is empty, a passkey from firstname, lastname and date of birth will be generated or as fallback the email or extId is used.
passkeyUsername.x-comment: Hint: the user can see this key on the screen.

case.title: Qualifier to define the case that should be used. For QES, this field is deprecated (use process steps instead).
case.x-comment: Used for example by bank avera. But DEPRECATED for QES, use processSteps field instead.

processSteps.title: The sequence of the fidentity process steps can be specified individually.

noValidation.title:
Defines if the process is blocked when validation fails
            noValidation = true > process is not blocked
            noValidation = false > process is blocked

showCloseTabMessageAfterNFC.title: Flag that defines if a message to close the browser tab should be shown after NFC scanning. Only relevant if the process is started on mobile.

selfie.title: Flag that defines if selfie process should be used. This field is deprecated. Use process steps instead.

type.title: type of document, values are used to distinguish the document types

certificate.title: The public certificate of the signee (base64)

preparedDigest.title: The hash of the pkcs7 object (base64)

process.title: The overall status of the process. If no review state is set, then the process state has the value of the system state. If a review state is set, then the process state has the value of the last review state.

system.title: This value is defined by fidentity based on all process steps and cannot be changed

review.title: This value is defined manually in the admin gui by a reviewer. The value can be changed several times

log.title: process validation log. ! to be treaded as log and not to be parsed or validated ! Use verification checkpoints to get overview and scores of validation parts.

assessor.title: assessment number, date and company
assessor.example: No. 207 / 2023 as of 21012023 done by KPMG AG

documentUris.title: DEPRECATED, use mediaUris instead, Document uris

key.title: A key for the process step

type.title: Defines the module that should be used in this process step.

subtype.title: The subtype of the process step. Use "nfc" in case of NFC Chip validation.

documentType.title: The document type. Is used for additional document types (e.g. studentCard).

state.title: The state for this process step.

verifiedValue.title: Value verified by a review agent

key.title: A key for the process step

type.title: Defines the module that should be used in this process step.

subtype.title: The subtype of the process step. Use "nfc" in case of NFC Chip validation.

documentType.title: The document type. Is used for additional document types (e.g. studentCard). Can be used in step "DocumentRegistration" and "PdfScan".

systemUrls.title: Section to define callbacks or redirect URLs

items.title: System URL for callbacks and redirects

type.title: type of url. Callback url is called after the process is finished. After finishing process user is redirected to redirect url. Review callback url is called after the process is manually reviewed.

subtype.title: subtype of url. Currently only used for the type 'redirect' and state 'review-pending'

state.title: defines the status of the process when calling the url

template.title: Template to customize a body for a callback request

headers.title: Additional headers for the callback request

hashesToSign.title: section for hashes that will be signed electronically

type.title: type of document, values are used to distinguish the document types

hashAlgorithm.title: The hash algoritm used to create the document hash

documentLink.title: A (external) link to the corresponding document. Please contact fidentity to enable this feature since it needs special configuration.

inputDocuments.title: section for documents that will be signed electronically

type.title: type of document, values are used to distinguish the document types

flatten.title: If true, the document will be flattened. This means that all form fields will be removed and the document will be saved as a flat PDF. This is useful for signing documents that contain form fields.

oneOf.title: section to position the electronic signature

x.title: The x position from the left in user space units (distance in inch*72)

y.title: The y position from the top in user space units (distance in inch*72)

page.title: The page index, starting at 0 for first page. Use -1 to place the signature on the last page.

oneOf.title: Placeholder string to position the electronic signature. This feature is only available if "Signature" type is set in processSteps.

placeholder.title: Instead of coordinates and page number you can pass a placeholder string. The signature will be inserted at the first occurence of the placeholder in the pdf document. The placeholder in the pdf needs to be between 2 dollar signs: $$Signature-0001$$. However you must only pass the placeholder string to the request, without dollar signs. The placeholder in the pdf may also contain the desired width and height of the signature box as well as the desired signature text. Example1: $$Signature-0001,100,50$$ (Placeholdername, width, height) Example2: $$Signature-0001,100,50,Signed by John Doe$$ (Placeholdername, width, height, signature text).

visualSignature.title: section to define the visual size of the electronic signature

text.title: The text that will be displayed on the signature. Supports the following placeholders: {{date}}, {{time}},  {{datetime}} with optional format {{date | dd.MM.yyyy}}, {{time | HH:mm:ss}}, {{datetime | yy-MM-DDTHH:mm}}
text.example: Signed by fidentity (this is the default if no custom text is passed)

image.title: The base64 encoded image as Data Url (data:[<mediatype>][;base64],<data>) that will be displayed on the signature. Encoded, it should be no larger than 200Kb. Will be resized to fit the signature box. If you want the handwritten Signature as the visual Signature, you must pass an empty string and add the HandwrittenSignature Step to the processSteps array

boxSize.title: Total size of the visual signature box, containing the signature text and the image (in user space units = inches*72. For example: 1 inch = 2.54 cm = 72 user space units)

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description:
fidentity offers the following form of authentication:
- OAuth2: OAuth2 is an open protocol to allow secure authorization in a simple and standard method from web, mobile and desktop applications.

tags.name: fidentity
tags.description: Start fidentity process and receive its data
